mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate remains near zero while inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 8% year-over-year, creating negative real rates that erode equity risk premiums and compress forward multiples. This regime produces negative S&P 500 returns as investors demand higher nominal yields to compensate for purchasing power loss.
evidence_refs: fact:May 2022 inflation at 8.6% YoY with Fed funds at 0.4%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where trigger condition is met, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 18-month rolling window during high-inflation low-rate regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) over 3 consecutive months signals weakening labor demand and rising recession risk, triggering equity sell-offs as investors reprice earnings expectations downward. The mechanism operates through forward-looking recession probability, not contemporaneous unemployment.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 100k in any single month OR 3-month moving average growth turns negative
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 144,952k in April 2022, near cycle highs
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with early recession signals
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in > 70% of instances where PAYEMS declines exceed 100k in a month, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 2% year-over-year while remaining positive, indicating consumer demand is weakening but not collapsing. This intermediate state produces equity uncertainty and sideways returns as the market reprices growth expectations from expansion to stagnation.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS YoY growth 0% to 2% AND prior 3-month trend is downward
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns exceed +2% in > 60% of instances where RSAFS growth is between 0-2% YoY with downward trend, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth remains positive but decelerates to below 3% year-over-year, signaling supply-side constraints are easing while demand remains soft. This creates a favorable regime for equity returns as margin pressure from supply bottlenecks diminishes without demand collapse.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of observations where INDPRO YoY growth is between 0-3%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline more than 15% month-over-month while unemployment (UNRATE) rises simultaneously, creating a joint signal of demand destruction and labor market deterioration. This interaction predicts negative equity returns as both forward earnings and discount rates move unfavorably.
candidate_trigger: HOUST MoM decline > 15% AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in same month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,047k in March 2022, UNRATE at 4.9% in April 2022
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of instances where both HOUST declines > 15% MoM and UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate rises above 1.5% within a 3-month window from near-zero levels, signaling aggressive monetary tightening. This rapid rate shock compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduces refinancing availability for leveraged corporate structures, producing negative forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.4% in April 2022, with Fed signaling rapid hikes
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns exceed +1% in > 70% of instances where FEDFUNDS rises > 1.5pp in 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) remains below 5% while nonfarm payroll growth (PAYEMS) continues at > 200k per month, indicating a tight labor market with wage pressure. This regime produces positive equity returns as strong employment supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, offsetting inflation concerns.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.9% and PAYEMS at 144,952k in April 2022
regime_hint: tight labor market with strong demand
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of instances where UNRATE < 5% and PAYEMS growth > 200k, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized while retail sales (RSAFS) remain positive, indicating an economy approaching stall-speed. This regime produces negative equity returns as investors reprice terminal growth rates and shift to defensive positioning.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth < 1.5% AND RSAFS YoY growth > 0%
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns exceed +2% in > 65% of instances where GDPC1 growth < 1.5% annualized and RSAFS growth remains positive, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.
